description: Fix window.fetch being permanently patched with no teardown: the Spotify plugin intercepts window.fetch to capture Bearer tokens but never restores the original after deactivation or adapter teardown.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/spotify && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Restore window.fetch in teardown() when the Spotify adapter is deactivated
userStories.description: In plugins/spotify/src/spotify-api.ts, installInterceptor() replaces window.fetch with a wrapper that captures Bearer tokens (line 53, called at module load). The original fetch is saved to globalThis.__spotifyOrigFetch. However, the plugin class in plugins/spotify/src/index.ts has no teardown() method, so window.fetch stays patched permanently after the adapter is deactivated or reloaded. Add a teardown() method to the plugin class that restores the original fetch and resets the interceptor state.
acceptanceCriteria: The SpotifyPlugin class in index.ts implements a teardown() method
teardown() restores window.fetch to the value stored in globalThis.__spotifyOrigFetch
teardown() deletes globalThis.__spotifyOrigFetch and resets interceptorInstalled to false so a subsequent installInterceptor() call works correctly
After teardown(), window.fetch is the original browser fetch (not the Spotify interceptor wrapper)
